title: Protocol for Quantitative Estimation of Hydrogen Cyanide Production from Bacteria
author: Devashish Pathak
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

steps.title: Luria-Bertani (LB) broth Reagent Quantity or...
steps.instruction: Luria-Bertani (LB) broth Reagent Quantity or volume (per liter) Tryptone 10 g Yeast extract 5 g NaCl 10 g Distilled water up to 1 L Adjust pH to 7.

steps.title: Autoclave at 121°C for 15 min
steps.instruction: Autoclave at 121°C for 15 min.

steps.title: King’s B broth (supplemented with glycine)...
steps.instruction: King’s B broth (supplemented with glycine) Reagent Quantity or volume (per liter) Proteose peptone 20 g Glycerol 10 mL K 2 HPO 4 1.5 g MgSO 4 ·7H 2 O 1.5 g Distilled water Up to 1 L Adjust pH to 7.

steps.title: Autoclave at 121°C for 15 min
steps.instruction: Autoclave at 121°C for 15 min. Add filter-sterilized glycine to a final concentration of 4.4 g/L after autoclaving. Dispense 10 mL into each sterile test tube under aseptic conditions.

steps.title: Alkaline picrate solution Reagent Quantity or volume Picric acid 2
steps.instruction: Alkaline picrate solution Reagent Quantity or volume Picric acid 2.5 g Na 2 CO 3 12.5 g Double-distilled water (ddH 2 O) Up to 1,000 mL Mix both components thoroughly until fully dissolved. Store in a tightly closed amber bottle at room temperature.

steps.title: Potassium ferrocyanide standard solutions
steps.instruction: Potassium ferrocyanide standard solutions

steps.title: Use K 4 [Fe(CN) 6 ]...
steps.instruction: Use K 4 [Fe(CN) 6 ] as a standard to prepare 0–50 ppm of CN ion equivalents. b. Use the following molecular conversion: 422.4 g potassium ferrocyanide = 156 g of CN- c. Prepare standards in 10 mL of ddH 2 O per tube (e.g., 0, 10, 20, 30, 40, and 50 ppm CN equivalents). d. Cover immediately to prevent evaporation or contamination. Laboratory supplies Note: Prepare and autoclave sufficient double-distilled water (ddH 2 O) and ensure availability of sterile test tubes, conical flasks, forceps, scissors, and pipettes.

steps.title: Sterile culture tubes or conical flasks...
steps.instruction: Sterile culture tubes or conical flasks (HiMedia, catalog numbers: PW1233 and LA449

steps.instruction: Sterile measuring cylinders, conical flasks, and beakers (HiMedia, catalog numbers: LA454, LA449, and LA450

steps.title: Sterile filter paper (Whatman No
steps.instruction: Sterile filter paper (Whatman No. 1 or equivalent), cut into 12 × 1 cm strips (HiMedia, catalog number: MB

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: For filter paper strip preparation, first cut Whatman No. 1 or equivalent filter paper into 12 × 1 cm strips, marking at 10 cm using a pencil. Sterilize strips via autoclaving (by keeping inside the autoclavable bag). Soak sterilized strips in alkaline picrate solution for 1–2 min. Let excess drip off in sterile conditions (e.g., under a laminar flow hood). Use immediately or store in sterile, sealed containers.

steps.title: Sterile Suba seal or rubber stopper (HiMedia, catalog number
steps.instruction: Sterile Suba seal or rubber stopper (HiMedia, catalog number: LA

steps.title: 0.22 μm syringe filter (HiMedia, catalog number
steps.instruction: 0.22 μm syringe filter (HiMedia, catalog number: ME

steps.title: Parafilm, (HiMedia, catalog number
steps.instruction: Parafilm, (HiMedia, catalog number: PW

steps.title: Sterile forceps and scissors (HiMedia, catalog numbers
steps.instruction: Sterile forceps and scissors (HiMedia, catalog numbers: LA396 and LA

steps.title: Sterile pipettes and tips (HiMedia, catalog numbers
steps.instruction: Sterile pipettes and tips (HiMedia, catalog numbers: PW1149 and MBP001–MBP003)

steps.title: Inoculate the purified bacterial strain into...
steps.instruction: Inoculate the purified bacterial strain into pre-prepared LB broth and incubate overnight at 28 ± 2 °C in a shaker incubator (150–180 rpm) to allow active growth. Maintain a blank in the same conditions.

steps.title: Dispense 10 mL of King’s B...
steps.instruction: Dispense 10 mL of King’s B broth (with glycine) into sterile test tubes under aseptic conditions.

steps.title: Transfer 1% (v/v) of the overnight-grown...
steps.instruction: Transfer 1% (v/v) of the overnight-grown culture into each test tube containing King’s B broth and incubate until the culture reaches an OD 600 ≈ 1.0, indicating idiophase (secondary metabolite production phase). B. Paper strip handling and setup

steps.title: Using sterile forceps, take the alkaline...
steps.instruction: Using sterile forceps, take the alkaline picrate–soaked filter strips and hang them vertically inside each test tube, ensuring 10 cm of the strip remains inside the headspace above the bacterial broth without touching the liquid ( Figure 1 ).

steps.title: Seal the tubes tightly with Suba...
steps.instruction: Seal the tubes tightly with Suba seals or rubber stoppers and further wrap the seals with Parafilm to prevent gas leakage.

steps.title: Incubate the sealed tubes at 28...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sealed tubes at 28 ± 2 °C for 14 days without disturbance. Figure

steps.title: Insertion of the strip and sealing...
steps.instruction: Insertion of the strip and sealing it with Suba seals and Parafilm C. Standard curve assay (parallel setup)

steps.title: Place pre-treated alkaline picrate filter strips...
steps.instruction: Place pre-treated alkaline picrate filter strips inside sterile test tubes containing 10 mL of potassium ferrocyanide standard solutions (ranging from 0 to 50 ppm CN- equivalents).

steps.title: Seal and incubate these tubes under...
steps.instruction: Seal and incubate these tubes under the same conditions as for the test culture (28 ± 2 °C for 14 days).

steps.title: After incubation, carefully cut each strip...
steps.instruction: After incubation, carefully cut each strip at the 10 cm mark using sterile scissors and place it into 10 mL of ddH 2 O in a clean tube or beaker.

steps.title: Gently shake or vortex the tubes...
steps.instruction: Gently shake or vortex the tubes to fully elute the color from the strip, forming the isopurpurate compound. D. Color elution from bacterial samples

steps.title: Repeat the same cutting and elution...
steps.instruction: Repeat the same cutting and elution process (as in step C

steps.title: for strips recovered from bacterial culture tubes and the blank
steps.instruction: for strips recovered from bacterial culture tubes and the blank.

steps.title: Ensure equal volume and shaking conditions...
steps.instruction: Ensure equal volume and shaking conditions are maintained for all samples ( Figure 2 ). Figure

steps.title: Hydrogen cyanide (HCN) production assays
steps.instruction: Hydrogen cyanide (HCN) production assays. (A) Color development for different isolates. (B) Color elution from filter paper at different concentrations of potassium ferrocyanide. (C) Eluted color of isopurpurate. E. Measurement and quantification

steps.title: Measure the absorbance (OD) at 625...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance (OD) at 625 nm for each eluted solution using a spectrophotometer.

steps.title: Plot a standard curve using OD...
steps.instruction: Plot a standard curve using OD values from the potassium ferrocyanide standards against known CN- concentrations.

steps.title: Use this curve to estimate the...
steps.instruction: Use this curve to estimate the HCN concentration in each bacterial sample by correlating its OD value.

steps.title: Apply the derived linear regression formula (e
steps.instruction: Apply the derived linear regression formula (e.g., x = y/0.0013-0.0076 ) to calculate precise HCN production in ppm, where x = HCN concentration and y = OD 625 .

description: Validation of protocol 1. The absorbance values were compared with the standard curve of potassium ferrocyanide. The calculation of HCN concentration is based on the dissociation of potassium ferrocyanide. It dissociates into four K + ions and one [Fe (CN) 6 ] -4 ion, which further dissociates into six CN- ions in an aqueous condition [7]. Reaction equation: [ F e ( C N ) 6 ] K 4 · 3 H 2 O → [ F e ( C N ) 6 ] - 4 + 4 K + [ F e ( C N ) 6 ] - 4 ⇔ H + F e + 2 + 6 C N - → H + 6 H C N 2. It was observed that two-thirds of the cyanide ions were involved in the formation of isopurpurate, while one-third went into the formation of carbonate ions [8]. This can be represented by the equation: C 6 H 3 N 3 O 7 + 2 H C N + N a 2 C O 3 → C 8 H 2 O 6 N a N 5 + H 2 C O 3 + N a O H Therefore, taking OD without prior calculation of CN- ion from the standard compound would not show the real value of produced HCN. 3. The OD value obtained from the quantitative assay represents the amount of isopurpurate compound formed, which was directly related to the concentration of CN- ion calculated from potassium ferrocyanide, which represented the value of HCN produced ( Table 1 , Supplementary Table S1). Table 1. Quantitative value of HCN production of different isolates No. Isolates Organism HCN produced (ppm) Gram-positive bacteria 1 S1 Bacillus altitudinis 40.24 ± 0.15 2 PC4-29 Bacillus amyloliquefaciens 37.90 ± 0.81 3 PHM5-9 Bacillus haynesii 36.78 ± 0.40 Gram-negative bacteria 1 S4 Pantoea agglomerans 38.58 ± 0.96 2 PC4-31 Kosakonia cowanii 39.11 ± 0.94 3 PC4-48 Brucella intermedia 47.44 ± 0.49 3. The above-mentioned methodology is the next advancement in the old traditional method to quantify HCN production by bacterial isolates. This method includes the colorimetric test for the eluted color of the isopurpurate compound in double-distilled water. Through the standard curve of potassium ferrocyanide, we can get the exact concentration of cyanide for different intensities of color develop
